slides.title: The derivative of eˣ
slides.body: Drag the point along y = eˣ. Its slope triangle has a leg on the x-axis: the height drops straight down (the rise), and a run of 1 reaches the tangent's x-intercept. Since rise = height and run = 1, the slope equals the height itself. That's d/dx[eˣ] = eˣ.

slides.title: Differentiating any base: d/dx[nˣ]
slides.body: Any exponential nˣ can be rewritten with base e. Play the animation to see the chain rule pull out a factor of ln(n).

slides.title: Slope of eˣ
slides.body: Drag the tangent's x-intercept to build the slope triangle at the marked point. Its leg should sit on the x-axis with a run of 1. Then read off (or type) the slope of y = eˣ there.

feedback.wrong: For eˣ the tangent meets the x-axis one unit to the left, so run = 1 and the slope equals the height. Adjust the intercept, or type the slope.

slides.title: Reflecting to ln(x)
slides.body: Start on y = eˣ with its slope triangle, then reflect across y = x to get y = ln x. Watch the triangle flip: the rise becomes 1 and the run becomes the x-value, so the slope is 1/x.

slides.title: Slope of ln(x)
slides.body: Drag the tangent's y-intercept to build the slope triangle at the marked point. Its leg sits on the y-axis with a rise of 1. Then read off (or type) the slope of y = ln(x) there.

feedback.wrong: Not quite. Build the triangle so its rise is 1 on the y-axis (or type the slope), then check again.

slides.title: Polynomial playground: the product rule
slides.body: Build two polynomials u(x) and v(x), then play the animation to watch the product rule derive (uv)′ = u′v + uv′ on your own functions.

slides.title: Apply the product rule
slides.body: You are given u(x) and v(x). Work the product rule in three steps, building each polynomial.

feedback.wrong: Differentiate each factor, then combine with (uv)′ = u′v + uv′ and simplify.

slides.title: Quick check: exponentials
slides.body: Use the rule for differentiating any base.

feedback.wrong: Rewrite 3ˣ as e^(ln(3)·x); the chain rule brings down a factor of ln(3).

slides.title: Quick check: product rule
slides.body: Expand the product, then build the derivative in standard form.

feedback.wrong: Use (uv)′ = u′v + uv′ with u = x + 2 and v = 3x − 1, then simplify.
